TYPICAL PEDON.content:
Typical Pedon:
Globeville sand clay loam in urban, park. (Colors are for dry soil unless otherwise noted.)
^A--0 to 10 cm; brown (10YR 4/3) sandy clay loam, dark brown (10YR 3/3) moist; moderate fine granular structure; soft, very friable, slightly sticky, slightly plastic; common fine roots, 2 percent gravel, noneffervescent, neutral (pH 7.2); clear smooth boundary.
^Cu--10 to 28 cm; dark grayish brown (10YR 4/2) sandyclay loam, very dark grayish brown (10YR 3/2) moist; massive; slightly hard, friable, slightly sticky, slightly plastic; common fine roots; common, fine and medium prominent yellowish brown (10YR 5/6 moist, mottles (relict redoximorphic iron concentrations, assumed to have been inherited from the fill); 2 percent gravel; 1 percent gravel-sized artifacts of brick and 2 percent gravel-sized artifacts of metal; noneffervescent; neutral (pH 7.0); clear smooth boundary.
^C1--28 to 42 cm; dark grayish brown (10YR 4/2)sandy clay loam, very dark grayish brown (10YR 3/2) moist; massive; slightly hard, friable, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; few fine roots; common, fine and medium prominent yellowish brown (10YR 5/6) moist, mottles (relict redoximorphic iron concentrations, assumed to have been inherited from the fill); 2 percent gravel; noneffervescent; neutral (pH 7.2); clear smooth boundary.
^C2--42 to 55 cm; brown (10YR 5/3)sandy clay loam, dark grayish brown (10YR 4/2) moist; massive; moderately hard, firm, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; few fine roots; common, fine and medium prominent yellowish brown (10YR 5/6) moist, mottles (relict redoximorphic iron concentrations, assumed to have been inherited from the fill); 2 percent gravel; noneffervescent; neutral (pH 7.2); clear smooth boundary.
^C3--55 to 82 cm; yellowish brown (10YR 5/4)sandy clay loam, brown (10YR 4/3) moist; massive; slightly hard, friable, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; few fine roots; noneffervescent; neutral (pH 7.2); clear smooth boundary.
2C1--82 to 166 cm; pale brown (10YR 6/3) loamy coarse sand, yellowish brown (10YR 5/4) moist; single grain; loose, loose, non-sticky, non-plastic;  few fine roots; finely disseminated free carbonates and common, medium, irregular, non-cemented carbonate masses throughout the matrix; strongly effervescent; moderately alkaline (pH 8.2); gradual wavy boundary.
^2C2--166 to 200 cm; pale brown (10YR 6/3) loamy sand, yellowish brown (10YR 5/4)moist; single grain; loose, loose, non-sticky, non-plastic;  few fine roots finely disseminated free carbonates and common, medium, irregular, non-cemented carbonate masses throughout the matrix; strongly effervescent; moderately alkaline (pH 8.2).
Type Location:
Denver County, CO
USGS Quadrangle: Arvada
Latitude: 39.7625194
Longitude: -105.0262556
Datum: WGS84
Coordinates sources: Hand-held GPS
Range in Characteristics:
Depth to free carbonates--75 to 150 centimeters
Thickness of human-transported material--50 to 200 centimeters
Particle-size control section (weighted average):
Clay content--18 to 30 percent
Sand content--35 to 65 percent
Total rock fragments--0 to 15 percent
^A or ^Au horizon(s):
Hue--7.5YR to 10YR
Value--3 to 5 dry; 1 to 3 moist
Chroma--3 or 4 dry; 2 to 4 moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Clay loam, loam, sandy loam
Clay content--18 to 30 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 5 percent gravel
Artifacts--0 to 3 percent
Reaction--pH 7.0 to 7.6
Thickness--5 to 20 centimeters
^Cu horizon(s) (where present):
Hue--10YR
Value--4 or 5 dry; 3 or 4 moist
Chroma--3 or 4; dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--clay loam, sandy clay loam, sandy loam
Clay content--20 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 25 percent gravel
Artifacts--1 to 10 percent
Reaction--pH 7.0 to 7.6
^C horizon(s):
Hue--10YR
Value--4 to 6 dry; 3 to 5 moist
Chroma--3 to 5; dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--clay loam, sandy clay loam, sandy loam
Clay content--20 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 15 percent gravel
Reaction--pH 7.0 to 7.6
Thickness--15 to 40 centimeters
Thickness-10 to 50 centimeters
2C horizon(s) (when present):
Hue--10YR
Value--5 or 6 dry or moist
Chroma--4 or 5 dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Clay loam, sandy loam, loamy sand
Clay content--5 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 10 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--0 to 14 percent
Reaction--pH 7.4 to 8.2
Thickness--5 to 35 centimeters
Competing Series:
There are currently no competing series.
Geographic Settings:
Parent material-- Thick and very thick fine-loamy human-transported material over alluvium or eolian deposits derived from mixed lithologies
Landform-- Human-transported fill on Holocene-aged stream terraces or plains mantled with eolian deposits
Slope ranges--0 to 5 percent
Mean annual air temperature-10.0 to 12.0 degrees C (50.0 to 53.6 degrees F)
Mean annual precipitation range--356 to 432 millimeters (14.0 to 17.0 inches)
Frost-free period--135 to 165 days
Elevation--1,565 to 1,720 meters (~5,135 to 5,645 feet)
Climate--continental, temperate, semi-arid; very cold and snowy winters; cool, moist springs, hot summers, with North American monsoon precipitation from July through August.
Geographically Associated Soils:
Southplatte soils: Southplatte soils are found on adjacent plains and have a cambic horizon.
Drainage and Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity:
Drainage class: Well and moderately well drained
Saturated hydraulic conductivity: Moderately high (1.5 to 5 mm/sec)
Use and Vegetation:
Most areas are primarily used for urban development. The potential native vegetation is fourwing saltbush, winterfat, western wheatgrass, and blue grama.
Distribution and Extent:
Eolian deposit-mantled plains and Holocene-aged stream terraces, primarily on the east bank of the South Platte River near Denver, CO; small extent in north-central Colorado; MLRA 67B.
Soil Survey Region (SSR) Responsible:
Bozeman, Montana
Series Proposed:
Denver County, CO 2025. Globeville is the name of neighborhood in the City and County of Denver, Colorado
Remarks:
Typical Pedon Taxonomic Features:
Ochric epipedon--0 to 22 centimeters (^A horizon)
Human-transported materials--0 to 78 centimeters (^A, ^C, and ^Cu horizons)
Artifacts--47 to 78 centimeters (^Cu horizon)
Lithologic discontinuity-- 78 centimeters
Particle-size control section--25 to 100 centimeters
Taxonomic version: Keys to Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition, (2022)
